title: Naturama

jokes.text: Mutual of omicron... insuring your world, and its destruction.
jokes.setup: Standard insurance company tagline about protecting your world
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions they also insure its destruction
jokes.explanation: Dark subversion of corporate speak with efficient delivery

jokes.text: I'm born, baby!
jokes.setup: Documentary narrator describing salmon eggs hatching
jokes.punchline: Baby salmon announces his birth with human enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven humor with Fry's signature enthusiasm

jokes.text: What's your name? I don't have a name. I'm a salmon.
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact response highlighting the absurdity
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective absurdist exchange

jokes.text: I do look forward to cavorting in the spray.
jokes.punchline: Unexpectedly sophisticated vocabulary for a fish
jokes.explanation: Mild character humor from vocabulary mismatch

jokes.text: Let's all ten of us go together. There's safety in numbers.
jokes.setup: Narrator just said only 3 in 10 will survive
jokes.punchline: Salmon optimistically plan to all stay together
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony as audience knows their fate

jokes.text: I reckon my boy will be right pleased when I regurgitate that into his mouth.
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions disgusting but natural feeding method
jokes.explanation: Mild gross-out humor from natural behavior

jokes.text: Whoa! Oh my! Oh dear! Oh yes!
jokes.punchline: Hedonismbot's characteristic sexual exclamations during fall
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration - Hedonismbot finding pleasure even in mortal peril

jokes.text: This just in. Three dead in foamy plunge.
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact news report treating it like breaking news
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition of nature doc with news format

jokes.text: Still, six out of ten alive. That's considered good for our species! Five out of ten, also nice.
jokes.punchline: Optimistic spin despite worsening odds
jokes.explanation: Dark humor from maintaining optimism despite mounting deaths

jokes.text: birthplace of all life. Except the salmon.
jokes.setup: Grandiose statement about ocean being birthplace of all life
jokes.punchline: Casual scientific correction
jokes.explanation: Good documentary parody with dry correction

jokes.text: I'd like to mate with you in a few years when we're sexually mature. I'd like that.
jokes.punchline: Bluntly biological approach to romance
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor from overly clinical romance

jokes.text: Coho, what have we here? I'm a pieces. And you? Also pieces. We're all pieces.
jokes.punchline: Terrible fish pun on 'Pisces'
jokes.explanation: Weak pun that relies on mispronunciation without much payoff

jokes.text: You haven't ignored the last of me...
jokes.punchline: Botched threatening phrase
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from mangled threatening phrase

jokes.text: So how do we find our way home? Nobody knows!
jokes.punchline: Cheerfully unhelpful response
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor from unhelpful certainty

jokes.text: Okay, everybody do one of those three things.
jokes.setup: Narrator lists three possible navigation methods
jokes.punchline: Practical but absurd instruction to just pick one
jokes.explanation: Smart parody of nature doc uncertainty with practical solution

jokes.text: I can tell by the magnets in my head, or whatever.
jokes.setup: Scientific explanation of magnetic field navigation
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of amazing biological ability
jokes.explanation: Good casual dismissal of remarkable natural ability

jokes.text: Stupid body! Why won't you go to that other stream? Nobody knows!
jokes.punchline: Callback to earlier 'nobody knows' response
jokes.explanation: Good callback that builds on earlier joke structure

jokes.text: I just told you! Goodbye!
jokes.setup: Repeated 'nobody knows' response ignoring narrator's explanation
jokes.punchline: Frustrated narrator breaks fourth wall
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor breaking documentary format with frustrated narrator

jokes.text: I need a mate who's within squirting distance. I'll never forget you... fish.
jokes.punchline: Clinical biological terminology mixed with forgetting her name
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor mixing romance with biological reality

jokes.text: Come on, loxy lady, it's time for us to get naughty by nature!
jokes.punchline: Terrible salmon pun and awkward pickup line
jokes.explanation: Weak pun and cliched pickup line combination

jokes.text: Son of a fish!
jokes.punchline: Fish-appropriate version of common expletive
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective species-appropriate swear word

jokes.text: Jumping is for suckers.
jokes.setup: Salmon choosing easy path around waterfall
jokes.punchline: Immediately gets eaten by bear
jokes.explanation: Good setup and immediate ironic payoff with bear attack

jokes.text: Oh, I'm good! Who wants a piece of me? / I'll try a bite!
jokes.punchline: Bear immediately responding to rhetorical challenge literally
jokes.explanation: Nice setup-punchline with bear taking rhetorical question literally.

jokes.text: Okay, who's next? There's a lot more where that came from!
jokes.punchline: Treating biological function like a performance
jokes.explanation: Good character humor from Fry's enthusiasm about biological processes

jokes.text: I refuse to release anything unless it's in the vicinity of the eggs of the fish I love!
jokes.punchline: Fry's romantic approach to biological function
jokes.explanation: Strong character moment showing Fry's romantic nature even as fish

jokes.text: Tell my offspring I loved me... Very much...
jokes.punchline: Narcissistic final words instead of loving message
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of deathbed cliche with unexpected narcissism

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. Plop those roundies.
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual commentary on biological process
jokes.explanation: Crude humor with limited craft or character integration

jokes.text: I am brrr, devourer of fish and honey!
jokes.punchline: Bender's characteristic boasting as a bear
jokes.explanation: Good character integration with Bender's boastful personality as bear

jokes.text: What? It's spawning season! It's a bear holiday!
jokes.setup: Being criticized for eating too much
jokes.punchline: Treating natural feeding time as holiday excuse
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about seasonal eating patterns

jokes.text: That is what you said when you found those boy scouts! Next you'll be telling me I can't crap in the woods!
jokes.punchline: Dark escalation plus bear cliche reference
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with dark implications and cliche reference

jokes.text: You overcame salmon instinct and left your home stream for me!
jokes.punchline: Misunderstanding the situation completely
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's oblivious romanticism

jokes.text: And the best part is, it had a happy ending.
jokes.setup: Salmon about to die after spawning
jokes.punchline: Calling impending death a 'happy ending'
jokes.explanation: Good dark irony from Fry's optimistic misunderstanding of death

jokes.text: And so the endless circle of life comes to an end, meaningless and grim. Why did they live, and why did they die? No reason.
jokes.punchline: Nihilistic existential dread instead
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of nature doc optimism with existential bleakness

jokes.text: Also the blue-footed booby! That's fairly interesting!
jokes.punchline: Childish excitement over bird name
jokes.explanation: Mild juvenile humor from bird name reaction

jokes.text: Sad news, everyone. I'm lonesome.
jokes.setup: Dramatic introduction of last of species
jokes.punchline: Understated announcement of obvious situation
jokes.explanation: Good understated delivery of tragic situation

jokes.text: Would it? Would it? Would it?
jokes.punchline: Over-enthusiastic repetition
jokes.explanation: Mild character humor from over-eagerness

jokes.text: Except for the parasite!
jokes.punchline: Considering the victim's perspective
jokes.explanation: Good perspective shift considering overlooked party in symbiosis

jokes.text: A good beaking usually cheers me right up, but today it's no use.
jokes.punchline: Using 'beaking' as a service term like 'massage'
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay creating spa-like terminology for biological process

jokes.text: Hey, good lookin', I've got a lichen that needs a lickin'.
jokes.punchline: Terrible tortoise pickup line
jokes.explanation: Decent pun-based pickup line with species-specific elements

jokes.text: Ooh... talk about a cloaca shrinker.
jokes.punchline: Crude anatomical reference to attraction loss
jokes.explanation: Creative anatomically-specific reference for reptilian attraction loss

jokes.text: It's only 100 feet! At my age, it might as well be 200 feet.
jokes.punchline: Tortoise perspective on relative distance
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor about tortoise perception of distance

jokes.text: Well, let me think it over.
jokes.setup: Being urged to make quick decision
jokes.punchline: Long thoughtful pause typical of tortoises
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing tortoise deliberation pace

jokes.text: What brings yous to this part of the isleseses?
jokes.punchline: Stereotypical tough guy speech pattern
jokes.explanation: Standard tough guy character voice without much originality

jokes.text: Don't be such a Finch. We Galápagos tortoises have no natural predators.
jokes.punchline: Species name as insult plus factual reassurance
jokes.explanation: Decent species-based pun with educational element

jokes.text: That's so fascinating!
jokes.punchline: Amy's characteristic enthusiasm for mundane facts
jokes.explanation: Good character integration with Amy's enthusiastic personality

jokes.text: I'm not Fry. I'm his great-great grandson... Fry.
jokes.punchline: Evolution explanation ending with same name
jokes.explanation: Smart joke about evolution timeline vs. naming conventions

jokes.text: It evolved a slightly bigger hook over the generations, to help me eat the cactus on this part of the island. Watch! I'm stuck!
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrates disadvantage
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic demonstration of evolution's imperfection

jokes.text: You haven't aged a day! Should we say something? Nah. Let him have his fun.
jokes.punchline: She's clearly a skeleton, but finches let him enjoy it
jokes.explanation: Dark visual comedy with kind-hearted finches enabling delusion

jokes.text: What the tropical hell are you doing?!
jokes.setup: Hubert attempting to mate with skeleton
jokes.punchline: Geographic-specific swear word from angry tortoise
jokes.explanation: Good location-specific expletive in uncomfortable situation

jokes.text: I'll kill her! I'm going to crack that hussy open like a slutty Brazil nut!
jokes.punchline: Species-appropriate violent threat
jokes.explanation: Creative species-appropriate threat with good imagery

jokes.text: You always were a hot-blooded latina.
jokes.punchline: Reptile described as hot-blooded
jokes.explanation: Good biological irony since reptiles are cold-blooded

jokes.text: Like all reptiles, the Galápagos tortoise is cold-blooded.
jokes.punchline: Narrator's educational correction
jokes.explanation: Good educational callback correcting previous biological error

jokes.text: Um, should we be watching this? It seems kind of personal. That's not an issue for us. We're wildlife.
jokes.punchline: Wildlife perspective on privacy
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-commentary on nature documentary ethics

jokes.text: Just pretend you're eating seeds. Is it weird if I talk about his crazy turtle penis? No.
jokes.setup: Trying to act natural during mating
jokes.punchline: Casual discussion of anatomical details
jokes.explanation: Mild cringe humor from inappropriate commentary

jokes.text: Is it weird if I talk about his crazy turtle penis? / No.
jokes.setup: Finch asking if discussing tortoise anatomy is inappropriate
jokes.punchline: Casual acceptance of discussing animal genitalia
jokes.explanation: Mild cringe humor about nature documentary realities.

jokes.text: Oh, what do you want? I'm 150 years old.
jokes.punchline: Grumpy dismissal using age as excuse
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing post-coital grumpiness

jokes.text: Tortoises don't do that, do we? I sure as hell don't.
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of parental responsibility
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about reptilian parenting

jokes.text: Mama!
jokes.punchline: Calling out to absent parents before being eaten
jokes.explanation: Dark visual comedy of abandonment followed by predation

jokes.text: 200 million years of evolution, snuffed out. For in the end, nature is horrific, and teaches us nothing.
jokes.punchline: Nihilistic conclusion about meaninglessness
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to earlier nihilistic tone, subverting educational expectations

jokes.text: the hilarious antics of the elephant seal, The clown of the sea.
jokes.setup: Promising light-hearted content after dark segment
jokes.punchline: Dubious claim about elephant seal humor
jokes.explanation: Good transition humor setting up false expectations

jokes.text: Certainly not the salmon. And the tortoise? Yeah, right.
jokes.punchline: Dismissive references to previous segments
jokes.explanation: Good meta-callback referencing previous segments' failures

jokes.text: Aw, yeah! Survival of the fattest!
jokes.punchline: Bender's twist on 'survival of the fittest'
jokes.explanation: Good character integration with Bender's love of excess and clever wordplay

jokes.text: Form an orderly heap on top of me. I like mating with beachmaster 'cause he's the largest.
jokes.punchline: Clinical approach to massive group mating
jokes.explanation: Mild cringe humor from matter-of-fact mating preferences

jokes.text: I like mating with beachmaster 'cause he's the largest.
jokes.punchline: Reducing complex attraction to simple size measurement
jokes.explanation: Decent deadpan humor with simple logic, though somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Whoo! I'm doin' it in my sleep!
jokes.punchline: Boastful commentary on effortless success
jokes.explanation: Good Bender character moment of obnoxious boasting

jokes.text: Harem?! I can barely see 'em!
jokes.setup: Lesser males unable to attract mates
jokes.punchline: Vision problems preventing mate competition
jokes.explanation: Good age-related humor about mating disadvantages

jokes.text: Does this kelp scarf make me look fat enough?
jokes.setup: Lesser male trying to attract mates
jokes.punchline: Reverse body image concerns
jokes.explanation: Excellent reversal of typical body image insecurity

jokes.text: Bite my freshly molted, blubber-filled ass. I'm 40% ass!
jokes.punchline: Bender's signature phrase with species-specific twist
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character integration adapting signature catchphrase to seal anatomy

jokes.text: Once a lady goes walrus, she never... She ne... No one ever goes walrus.
jokes.punchline: Realizing mid-sentence that he's completely undesirable
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-aware humor with perfect timing on the realization

jokes.text: Well, while the beachmaster was off behind a whale carcass, loudly fornicating with my beloved mother, I snuck past and surprised my sweetie with a romantic meal.
jokes.setup: Asking how he managed sneaky mating strategy
jokes.punchline: Overly detailed story involving his mother's sexual encounter
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor from TMI about family sexual encounters, fits character type.

jokes.text: So, welcome to the abyss. Road trip!
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's cheerful attitude toward horrific environment
jokes.explanation: Good character integration with Zoidberg's enthusiasm for awful situations

jokes.text: Yeah, we can always make more. Come on, floozies, let's repopulate!
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of offspring death with crude proposition
jokes.explanation: Dark humor from callous attitude toward offspring mortality

jokes.text: I'm in love with you! And I don't care what he thinks. Also, who are you?
jokes.punchline: Admitting he doesn't know who she is
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist romance undermined by basic ignorance

jokes.text: Wanna do it? So soon?! Oh, well...
jokes.punchline: Male's fake reluctance quickly overcome
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from pretend moral hesitation

jokes.text: Well, nice try. Better luck next season. You gave it a shot! Pathetic.
jokes.setup: Catching sneaky male in the act
jokes.punchline: False encouragement followed by insult
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing condescending dominance

jokes.text: We beta males deserve better. When we're in charge, we'll treat females with respect, keeping sensible harems of no more than five.
jokes.punchline: Missing the point entirely about respectful treatment
jokes.explanation: Excellent satire of misguided 'nice guy' mentality

jokes.text: Beachmaster, stop fighting! He's been dead since yesterday! What?! He's dead?! Whoo! I'm killin' dudes in my sleep!
jokes.punchline: Beachmaster was fighting a corpse without realizing
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist escalation with callback to earlier 'in my sleep' boast

jokes.text: He's been dead since yesterday! / What?! He's dead?! / Whoo! I'm killin' dudes in my sleep!
jokes.punchline: Discovering he's been fighting a corpse and taking pride in it
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist moment with character's misplaced pride in pointless victory.

jokes.text: For a holographic brain injection of tonight's program, send five dollars to this station, care of me, the narrator.
jokes.punchline: Futuristic technology with personal profit motive
jokes.explanation: Good parody of documentary funding requests with sci-fi twist

tmdb_overview: Bender and the crew are reimagined as wild animals in a nature documentary.
